game.short_text: Death isn’t the end... it’s your only chance.
game.title: Dead Man's Draw

details.title: Dead Man's Draw
details.description:
Made For


About
Dead Man’s Draw is a fast-paced first-person shooter where death isn’t failure, it’s your only path forward.
You play as Jebediah Cross, a gunslinger cursed to ride forever in the Underwest, a desert purgatory where the dead duel for eternity. Stripped of your mortality, you’ve stolen Death’s own scythe and gained a terrible gift: each time you fall, you rise again in a state between life and death.
In this death state, you move faster, leap higher, and cannot be harmed... but you cannot kill. To extend your borrowed time, you must slay enemies while alive, harvesting their soul essence to fuel your next return to the grave. Run out, and you’ll be dragged back to your corpse, forced to fight again.
Only by mastering death itself can you cut through the witch’s gauntlet and reclaim your soul.

Key Features
Death as Progression – You can only complete levels while in your death state. Life is preparation, death is opportunity.
The Death State – Gain triple jump, boosted speed, and invulnerability while dead, but you can’t kill. Manage your soul essence to stay in the fight.
Soul Essence System – Slay enemies while alive to extend your death state timer. The more you reap, the longer you ride the line between life and death.
Deals with Death – Unlock new abilities at the end of each trial, customizing your movement with choices like dash, double jump, or slide.

You can find my main game project here.Unzip the folder and launch DeadMansDraw.exe.

game.short_text: Welcome to the afterlife’s HR department, your paperwork decides eternity.
game.title: Soul Ledger

details.title: Soul Ledger
details.description:
You died with sins still fresh on your record. Instead of instant fiery pits, you’ve been offered a deal, sort the souls of others, and maybe you’ll earn your own second chance.
Each file is a life, good deeds, bad habits, and suspicious excuses. Send them up, down, or spinning back to try again. Be careful, mistakes add up, and failure means your redemption arc ends where it began... back in torment.
(Sorry if the balancing is off, I tried my best to balance it within the time period given)
Known bugs: Heads up! When you reach an ending, your sound settings reset for the next new game. I’ll be fixing that after the game jam!
















Want to judge a soul? First, [E] while looking at the papers to pull up their paperwork. This shifts the camera to the paper, where you can then use the button on the left of the paperwork if it doesn't match the soul in front of you. 
If it matches you can then Press [E] while looking at the elevator buttons to shift the camera to them, then click to pick where the soul rides out eternity.
if you’re impatient in dialogue, you can use [E] again to skip all that dramatic typing.








This project was developed for Jamsepticeye 2025 by HoneyBellz, HoneyBellz is a solo dev who's goal was to create a game in under 5 days with the theme "Death is an Opportunity" and in this case the opportunity is to get a second chance, to prove that you are worthy of another attempt at life. In the game there are around 185 different things people in life can be know for, Some are completely wacky things made up on an extremely sleep deprived brain and other things are more grounded in reality, You'll have to decided if they really deserve eternal torment for a simple mistake or minor crime. 
There are currently 2 Endings in the game, However if there is enough support for the game I'd like to add several others including adding bribes to the game and an ending for that.
One of my biggest goals in the game was to not only make something repayable and easily expandable so that if I get enough support I can continue expanding on the game, but I also wanted to make something artistic and something I can be proud to show friends.
The main Inspo for the games art style was Tim Burton's character art I wanted something where the models seemed warped and distorted, My biggest boss fight making this game wasn’t a soul, it was coding. From creating my first save system to adding endings, tutorials, and teaching the 2D characters how to respect the 3D lighting and not glow in the dark, this was a real trial by fire.The game was completely made solo by HoneyBellz however I did use some free assets

game.short_text: A narrative exploration of the five stages of grief and death. A collaborative project for Jamsepticeye.
game.title: Strife of Vengeance

details.title: Strife of Vengeance
details.description:
Death is an opportunity. It is not the opposite of life, but rather a part of it. Death simply is. Death is inevitable. What if death was simply a beginning and not an ending? How far would you go to save someone you loved and lost?
Entering the Halls of Limbo, you take on the role of an Angel of Death working beneath Death herself, Queen of the Underworld. As the story progresses you'll find yourself facing familiar foes in the representations of the five stages of Grief. Strife of Vengeance is a dice-battler visual novel created in collaboration with Monarch, Lilivia, Ren_, Nikoheart, Kawa Soundforge, and Lusk as part of the first Jamsepticeye Game Jam.
--ALERT--
The dice-battler framework was first programmed during the Brackeys' jam this year. This project was iterated on top of it. Besides from the dice battler, every other system and even the art was done from scratch within the scope of the jam. We were told code reuse was legal as long as they were not specifically for this jam, and to the best of my understanding, this is valid within the rules.We are aware of a bug on the final fight with Death that causes a Soft Lock. Also, the ending is bugged currently if you defeat Death (you'll be taken to a Die selection screen and a curtain close). The Epilogue for the story has been added below.
--TUTORIAL--
Owing to time constraints, we could not add a tutorial, so please forgive us for adding it in the description:
The game itself is extremely simple. Drag the dice from the hand on the bottom of the screen to the slots present on the left side of the screen.
Your first die will clash will the enemy's first die, the second with the second, and so on. You deal the full roll damage to the enemy if your roll is greater.
If even one of the clashing dice are a "DEFENSIVE" type die, then the damage dealt is the difference of the rolls, not the full roll.
Instead of pressing the clash button you can also press Enter when you have all your dice slotted.--EPILOGUE--
There’s a pregnant pause in the air. It’s eerily quiet. For a moment I no longer hear the sounds of Limbo. I don’t feel the uneasy chill in the air. I take a breath and feel my lungs expand. Warmth. Hope. Joy. Happiness. I can feel the warmth of the sun on my face. A tear slips down my cheek as I slowly start remembering everything I’d forgotten while serving Death. I feel a head rub against my leg and look down to see my <PC Pet>. 
I smile, for the first time in a very long time, and I know that at least for now, everything will be alright and my life is mine again.
Thank you for playing Strife of Vengeance. You are the best audience we could have asked for. Thank you for playing our game.
Credits:
Art by: Lusk and Monarch
Programming by Monarch and Lilivia
Music by: Kawa SoundForge
Voice Acting by: Ren_ and Nikoheart
Writing by: Jess “RinnLiadon” Clements

game.title: Bizarre Tales: Deadly  Lookin' Pookie

details.title: Bizarre Tales: Deadly  Lookin' Pookie
details.description:
Death of your vision gives you the opportunity to see beyond.
Deadly Lookin’ Pookie is a first-person psychological horror game where you map your surroundings using a LIDAR scanner—and every scan risks revealing something you wish had stayed hidden.
 
You’re dropped inside the remains of a cult research compound once ruled by Grandma and her twisted idol — Popeye, the sailor turned saint of slaughter. Guided by a half-broken assistant,  you’ll piece together the story through Echo Notes and environmental clues — while avoiding things that should not exist.
Objectives
  Locate and collect 8 Echo Notes (voices trapped at death) to uncover the truth.
   Use LIDAR scans to reveal hidden paths, gate keys, and energy puzzles.
   Avoid The Lurker (visible only through scans) after 2 notes are collected.
   Avoid Popeye (visible only without scans) after 6 notes are collected.

Hints- The Gate key is outside on the right side of entrance, only visible in LiDAR mode- Some areas are visible only in LiDAR mode.- In Crystal Caves you need to fill the Crystal with LiDAR Scan.- In Furnace you need to find 3 engine Parts, visible on scanning, you need to scan till they appear in normal mode.

Hello new science team member! You've been tasked with helping revive a previously extinct species!

You're a new scientist at an advanced research facility. You've been tasked with evolving an previously extinct species.The creatures you'll care for are unstable, and will constantly mutate, and often die within minutes... and that's okay!
Every slime death is an opportunity to carry down mutations for future generations. You could take the long route and give each the care it needs... or you can focus on invasive research and experiments that may distress the creature, but will help advance the evolution faster.

Game created for the Jamsepticeye game jam! Thank you so much to Jacksepticeye and DuckyDevs for giving us the opportunity to work on this game <3
Content warning: This game explores dark themes, including self-sacrifice and death as narrative and gameplay elements. Some players may find these scenes distressing.

Note: There may be bugs since this is our very first game as a team. Possibly in the future we will patch them up. game.short_text: Live her life, learn her habits, and stay unseen in this Celtic changeling tale. Jamsepticeye Game Jam submission.
game.title: Síofra Sí

details.title: Síofra Sí
details.description:
"I am here now… It’s my turn."
Death can and will be an opportunity. Step into the life of Aisling, a recently departed girl, and play as a changeling learning to blend in. Explore her home, uncover her habits, and make fast, risky choices to survive interactions with her family. Every decision affects the suspicion meter: can you move through her world without being caught, or will something unseen reveal itself?
 
This is a fantasy story game inspired by Celtic and Irish mythology, made in 5 days for the Jamsepticeye Game Jam. It tells the story of a young changeling creature —a magical being that can take another’s skin — stepping into a human life, where observation and quick thinking are your only allies.
There are 4 unlockable endings.


Meet Aisling (The main character)!

Hints
Pay close attention to objects with a white outline as they will often hold clues about the “right” answer.If you get stuck, explore the rooms again. Aisling’s family and personal items usually reveal all the information you might need.Your choices affect the ending, so think quickly (but carefully) to avoid raising too much suspicion.Don’t forget the time limit: if you take too long to answer, the family becomes more suspicious, even more than if you choose the wrong answer.And lastly, wrong answers aren’t always obvious; They might even sound believable. game.short_text: Playing baccarat with Death!
game.title: Dealings With Death

details.title: Dealings With Death
details.description:
You find yourself exactly where you want to be: at a dirty table in the middle of utter nothingness, playing baccarat with Death. He will wager money against your life. The more you bet, the more he’ll give you. The less you have to bet, the higher the wager. Death is an opportunity, after all, and he wants you as close to it as possible. 
In Dealings With Death, you play a twisted version of baccarat against Death, trying to get a better hand than him. The closer to 9 your hand is, the better. But be careful, even 1 above 9 will loop you back to 0. 
But the odds aren’t all stacked against you. As you play, you will collect items to spend against Death. Items gather information, manipulate cards, and give you an advantage. But a bet is never 100%, as Death isn’t fair. He wins draws, so even if you have the perfect hand value of 9, there’s still a chance you lose. 
Death is inevitable, so you will eventually die. That matters not, as Death will keep a track of your money pot in his ledger. Maybe your next attempt will be better! 
This game was made in 3 and a half days for the Jamsepticeye 2025 Game Jam. Hope you enjoy! We're very eager for feedback, positive and negative, it's all helpful!Made in collaboration with MythicTy, OttoMoth, and Thomas Liddle
Also, send us a screenshot of your high score! If you beat this one, we'll add it here!
